value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

eSIM_2026.주요_제공사: Ubigi, Airalo, Saily, Mobal, Sakura Mobile, Holafly

disputes.nature: eSIM 보급으로 가격 구조 변화. 기존 물리SIM 가격 범위와 eSIM 가격 불일치

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

disputes.nature: 신제도 최소금액 — 기존 '철폐' 기재 vs 복수 출처(JNTO+japantravel) '5,000엔 유지' 확인
disputes.conclusion: 5,000엔(세전) 최소금액 유지 확인. 4개 독립 출처 일치. 기존 '철폐' 기재는 초기 보도 오류.

value.요약: JR Pass 이용 시 Hikari가 최적. Nozomi 대비 약 30-40분 추가 소요.

value: 도쿄 메트로 9개 노선 + 토에이 지하철 4개 노선 = 13개 지하철 노선. IC카드(Suica/PASMO) 사용 가능. 관광객용 Tokyo Subway Ticket으로 무제한 이용 가능. 자동 운임 계산. Welcome Suica/PASMO 이용 시 지하철+버스+편의점 결제 가능.

value.대상_노선: 도쿄 메트로 전 노선 + 토에이 지하철 전 노선

value.입실: 현관에서 신발 벗기. 석식 제공 시 18:00까지 도착 권장.
value.유카타: 실내 착용. 왼쪽이 위 (오른쪽 위는 고인용).
value.온천: 완전 탈의 필수, 입수 전 세신, 머리카락 물 접촉 금지, 수건 욕조 반입 불가. 남녀 분리 (교대 운영 가능).
value.매너: 22시 이후 소음 자제 (종이벽). 팁 불필요, 전달 시 봉투 사용.

value.오토시: 착석 시 자동 제공되는 작은 안주. 테이블 차지 겸용 300~700엔. 거절 불가.
value.주문법: 먼저 음료 주문 (맥주 관습). 메뉴 가리키기 OK. 터치패널 영어 전환 가능.

value.추천_시간: 이른 아침(일출) 또는 18시 이후 (혼잡 회피). 야간 등롱 조명.

value.방식: 환급형 — 구매 시 세금 포함 결제 → 출국 시 공항 세관에서 환급
절차: 4. 출국 시 공항 세관에서 여권+영수증+물품 확인

value.통용_브랜드: Visa, Mastercard (최광범위), AMEX, JCB, Diners Club, Discover
value.사용_가능: 주요 호텔, 백화점, 대형 쇼핑몰, 체인 레스토랑, 편의점, 관광지
value.현금_필요: 소규모 음식점 (라멘집, 가족경영), 노점/축제, 신사/사찰, 소도시 택시, 소규모 의원, 코인세탁소, 가챠폰
value.교통: 단거리 전철/지하철 요금은 카드 불가 → IC카드(Suica/PASMO) 권장
value.추세: 대도시 캐시리스 결제 급속 확대 중이나, 현금 항시 소지 권장

value.관광지할인: Enjoy Eco Card 제시 시 약 30개 관광지 할인

value.비고: 입장권이 부적(お札) 형태 — 기념품 겸용

value.IC카드: 대부분 지역 사용 가능. 일부 지방 예외 있음.
value.IC_락_주의: 하차역이 IC 미지원 시 카드 잠금. 주요 역에서 리셋 필요.
value.배차간격: 1~2시간 단위 가능. 사전 시간표 확인 필수.
value.팁: 현금 소액(동전) 준비 권장. IC 미지원 지역 대비.

value.특징: 다다미 방 + 이불, 공용 욕실, 가정 운영
value.체험: 농업 체험, 향토 요리 학습 등 가능

value.관광객용: Kansai One Pass — 관광지 할인 특전 포함

value.비고: 성수기/비수기 가격 차이 큼. 식사 포함 여부가 가격 좌우.

value.우체국ATM: 외국 카드 사용 가능. 주요 우체국.
value.환전: 공항이 가장 유리. 은행, 일부 편의점도 가능.
value.현금필요장소: 소규모 음식점, 노점, 지방 버스, 신사/사찰, 코인세탁소

value: 1 day $19, 2 days $32, 3 days $39, 4 days $47 (JR Kansai Area Pass)
1-day pass: ¥3,500; 2-day pass: ¥5,000; Sale period: March 25, 2025 – March 31, 2026 (1-day), March 25, 2025 – March 30, 2026 (2-day)

The Subway & Bus 1-Day Pass costs 1100 yen for adults and 550 yen for children aged 6-11, and allows unlimited trips for 1 day on Kyoto City Bus routes (including Sightseeing Limited Express Bus), Kyoto Municipal Subway lines, Kyoto Bus routes (some excluded), Keihan Bus routes (some excluded), and West Japan JR Bus routes (some excluded).
Price range for ryokans in Kyoto is $260 - $900 per night, including options like large family rooms.

To rent a car in Japan, you must be at least 18 years old and have an International Driving Permit (IDP) obtained in your country of residence before visiting Japan. A driver's license from your home country alone is not sufficient.
Collected info for tips from Osaka Food Tips Most Travelers Miss | Hidden Eats Guide
Available for 1, 2, 3 or 4 consecutive days

The Kansai Area Pass is available for tourists and travelers who want unlimited travel within the Kansai region, including major cities such as Osaka, Kyoto, Kobe, and Nara. It can be purchased either before arriving in Japan through accredited travel agencies or once in Japan at major JR West stations, Kansai International Airport, and selected travel agencies.

The Kansai Area Pass offers unlimited rides in major Kansai areas including Osaka, Kyoto, and Kobe, and is valid on WEST JAPAN JR BUS route buses within the valid area. It covers Limited Express, Express, Special Rapid, Rapid, and Local Services trains in the valid area, including the Kansai-airport Express HARUKA.
The Osaka Amazing Pass is available for durations of 1-day or 2-day.

The Osaka Amazing Pass is available only as a digital QR-code ticket since the physical card version ended in June 2024. It requires a valid credit card used for purchase and an internet-ready smartphone or tablet with a camera for use.
The Kansai Wide Area Pass can be purchased at JR West stations, major airports, and overseas.
Collected info for tips from Transit Guide: JR Kansai Area Pass - Japan Travel

The Osaka Amazing Pass is valid for either 1-day or 2-day durations, allowing unlimited use of Osaka subway, bus network, and private railways, plus free entry to around 40 iconic Osaka tourist attractions.
Kyoto city bus fare is planned to be lowered from 230 yen to 200 yen for Kyoto citizens, while noncitizens will pay 350 to 400 yen starting fiscal 2027
The Osaka Amazing Pass is available at subway and private railway stations such as Shinsaibashi, Namba (Midosuji, Sennichimae, Yotubashi, Kintetsu, Hanshin and Nankai).
The Osaka Amazing Pass offers unlimited rides on public transport and access to 40 attractions, costing 3,300 yen for one day and 5,500 yen for three days as of September 2024.
8–9 hours
Avoid the 207 and 206 bus routes in Kyoto if possible because these buses are jam packed with visitors, but there are many other buses that take the same routes.

The 10 most popular IC cards in Japan, including Suica, Pasmo, and Icoca, are compatible with each other and can be used on almost all train, subway, and bus lines inside Japanese cities. These IC cards can be purchased once in Japan and used alongside the Japan Rail Pass for seamless travel.
The Japan Rail Pass covers JR local buses, which can be identified by the JR logo on the bus and bus stops, but it no longer covers JR highway buses.
The Mobile Suica app is only available on the Japanese Google Play Store, and if your Android device supports FeliCa and Suica is visible in Google Wallet, you can set up a digital Suica card.

To use Takkyubin luggage forwarding service in Japan, fill in the shipping form with sender and recipient details, then drop off your luggage at a designated counter such as airports, hotels, or convenience stores.
Child (4-11 years old) ¥33,200 including tax; Universal Prime Annual Pass Standard - Adult (12 years old and over) ¥20,000 including tax
Tokyo is served by two airports: Narita International Airport (NRT) and Haneda Airport (HND). Haneda is closer and more convenient to Tokyo but has fewer international connections.
Private transfer from Narita Airport to Tokyo city center takes approximately 1.5 hours
Advance Ticket Tembo Deck Admission Tickets Weekdays: Adult ¥2,100, Teenager ¥1,400, Child ¥850; Combo Ticket Tembo Deck + Tembo Galleria Weekdays: Adult ¥3,100, Teenager ¥2,150, Child ¥1,300
Keisei Skyliner One-Way Ticket from Tokyo Narita Airport costs US$6.25 to US$6.95

Flat-rate fares from Haneda Airport to various Tokyo areas range from ¥6,900 (Chiyoda Area) to ¥12,800 (Musashino Area), with late night/early morning surcharges between ¥1,400 and ¥2,400 applied from 22:00 to 5:00

Tokyo Subway Tickets can be purchased at the following locations in Japan: Haneda Airport (International Terminal 2F Arrival Lobby Visitor Information Center), Narita Airport Terminal 1 (1F Arrival Lobby), Narita Airport Terminal 2 (1F Arrival Lobby Zone A and Zone B), Ueno Station Tourist Information, Ueno-okachimachi Station Tourist Information Center, Ginza Station Tourist Information, Shinjuku Station Tourist Information, Omote-sando Station Tourist Information, Tokyo Station Tourist Information, Ikebukuro Station Pass Office (Yurakucho Line), Iidabashi Station Pass Office, Takadanobaba Station Pass Office, Otemachi Station Pass Office, Ikebukuro Station Tourist Information (Yurakucho Line), Shinjuku Tourist Information, SAGAWA EXPRESS SHINJUKU SERVICE CENTER, H.I.S. Harajuku, WANDER COMPASS SHIBUYA, Ueno Information, Asakusa Culture Tourist Information Center, and Tourist Information Desk Asakusa.
Tokyo Metro operates 9 train lines covering 195 km of track between 180 stations, connecting major tourist destinations across Tokyo.
When purchasing a new Suica card, a 500-yen deposit is included in the price.
7–9 hours
Nagoya City Bus Regular Ticket Adult fare is 210 yen
When traveling in Japan, it is important to remove your shoes when entering homes and certain indoor places.

Essential Japanese travel phrases include greetings such as 'konnichiwa' (hello), 'ohayou gozaimasu' (good morning), 'hajimemashite' (nice to meet you), 'sayounara' (goodbye), 'onegaishimasu' (please), and 'arigatou gozaimasu' (thank you), with hiragana, kanji, and romaji provided along with pronunciation audio guides.
Japan's bus network is highly developed and efficient, offering a practical and economical alternative to trains and subways.

At many kaiten sushi restaurants in Japan, diners can order sushi individually using a tablet or touch panel, and the ordered sushi will be delivered directly to their table via the conveyor belt, accompanied by a chime sound to notify the customer.
For a 30 day rental, Japan Wireless is the cheapest option at ¥14,555

To use a capsule hotel, remove your shoes and place them into a locker, then take the key to the check-in counter. At check-in, you are assigned a capsule number and given a key to a locker for your belongings. Most people start with a bath and then change into fresh clothes. Capsules provide a private enclosed bed with basic amenities such as light, alarm clock, TV, radio, power outlets, and free Wi-Fi. Shared facilities include toilets, showers, and often large communal baths.
Universal Studios Japan park hours vary per day; for example, on some days the park operates from 8:30 AM to 9:30 PM
Each Suica is valid for 180 days from the issuance date
Welcome Suica cards are available at Haneda and Narita airports and at JR East Travel Service Centers in Tokyo, Shibuya, Shinjuku, Shinagawa, Ikebukuro, and Ueno stations.
Collected info for policy from Suica Cards: Quick Guide to Tokyo's Smart Travel Cards
2-1-33 Sakurajima, Konohana-ku, Osaka, 554-0031, Japan

Tokyo Skytree is open from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM on weekdays (Monday to Friday) with last admission at 9:00 PM, and from 9:00 AM to 10:00 PM on weekends (Saturday and Sunday) with last admission at 9:00 PM.
Sumida, Tokyo, Japan
Access to Super Nintendo World requires a separate Area Timed Entry Ticket, obtainable via the official USJ app, park kiosks, or included with certain Express Passes.
A seven-kilometer taxi ride in Tokyo takes about 25 minutes during the daytime.

To use a new SIM card in Japan, you need to have a Wi-Fi connection (such as airport free Wi-Fi) when installing the SIM card. Use airplane mode to turn off and then turn on your phone after inserting the SIM card.

In Tokyo, if no taxi is visible on the street, you can download a taxi app such as GO, Uber, or DiDi to hail a taxi. Registration on these apps is generally quick and easy, and enabling GPS on your smartphone is necessary for location tracking.
Tokyo Skytree offers online ticket sales through their official website and authorized ticketing platforms, allowing visitors to reserve tickets in advance and avoid long queues.
Major convenience stores in Japan, such as Lawson and FamilyMart, sell SIM cards and are open 24/7, making it convenient to purchase a SIM card.
Tokyo Subway Ticket is valid for unlimited travel on all Tokyo Metro and Toei Subway lines for 24 hours, 48 hours, or 72 hours from the travel starting time during the valid period.
Use Google Maps or the Uber app to estimate taxi fares in Tokyo before deciding if a taxi is worth it compared to public transport.
Airport SIM cards in Japan are generally overpriced compared to purchasing SIM cards at Bic Camera stores in town, which offer better deals.
Collected info for tips from Suica Cards: Quick Guide to Tokyo's Smart Travel Cards
